methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Amca is a robotics and manufacturing company at the IPO stage, founded by Jai Malik and Eli Giovanetti, having raised $76.5M to date. The valuation analysis operates within a limited peer universe: one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and seven private companies in robotics and manufacturing, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this analysis. The robotics and manufacturing sector contains only seven same-sector peers overall, but none satisfy the filtering criteria for reliable comparables. Specifically, zero peers exist at the same stage, zero at adjacent stages, and zero within similar valuation tiers. A median derived from all seven peers would be methodologically unsound, as it would conflate vastly different business maturity levels and capital structures. The threshold for statistical confidence—a minimum of three narrow-filtered datapoints—cannot be met.

This analysis is therefore constrained by insufficient comparable company data at Amca's IPO stage and valuation tier within its sector. No triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived from the available dataset. To support a credible IPO valuation, additional analysis would require either expansion of filtering criteria (accepting broader peer definitions), introduction of alternative methodologies (precedent transaction, DCF sensitivity), or access to unpublished financial metrics from the private peer set that would enable more granular staging and tier analysis.
